brief_justification: Paper A is a numerical linear-algebra scheme (subspace-iteration/QR projectors replacing truncated SVDs to accelerate CTMRG tensor-network contraction for iPEPS), whereas paper B is a statistical hypothesis test (a minimum-distance equivalence test for binary GLMs with bootstrap/asymptotic critical values). The governing machinery differs entirely — low-rank matrix approximation versus goodness-of-fit inference — with no role-for-role correspondence, so they share no underlying method.
